events.text: Step one. We'll generate a new ed25519 SSH key. ed25519 is the modern default: small, fast, well-supported. You only need to do this once across every server you'll ever add.
Step two. Copy the public key to your server with ssh-copy-id. Your server password is asked one last time. After this, password authentication is no longer required.
Step three. Now we register the key with rdc. From here on, every rdc command authenticates with this key.
Let's confirm the configuration shows the key path was stored correctly.
